---
title: "Fitting Hawkes processes on text-message data"
date: 2026-04-05
toc: true
---

## Abstract

## Introduction

<!-- Why Hawkes processes for messaging? What question does the project answer? -->

- This was a hammer looking for a nail.
- Interested in Hawkes processes, wanted to find something approachable to apply it to
- Definitely strong limitations

## Data

<!-- Source of timestamps, preprocessing, train/validation split, privacy considerations. -->

- Timestamp data from a Messenger chat, anonymized, no content included
- Picked most active chat
- Picked a random quarter from the past couple years to keep computation reasonable
- Timestamps might not be in my timezone
- Data didn't seem like it needed cleaning
- Immediate takeaways are daily cycle aspect, strong self-excitation, but also long periods of silence


## Models

<!-- Hawkes kernel(s), multivariate vs univariate, baseline intensity, identification. -->

- Exponential
- Sum Exponential
- Powerlaw
- Diurnal
- Fast Fourier Transform
- Hidden Markov Model

## Estimation and comparison

<!-- Likelihood / EM / MCMC or whatever you use; criteria for comparing specifications. -->

- Likelihood, just wanted as good of a fit as possible

## Results

<!-- Key figures and tables; what structure best fits bursts, reply chains, long-run rhythm. -->

- Everything with a powerlaw was able to fit the self-excitation aspect pretty well, but all failed to properly model end of conversation/silence periods
- Likely need a way to model latent variable or introduce more information to the model via either text message size (as a proxy for size, intensity of conversation) or message content (to figure out whether or not a conversation has run its course)


## Discussion and limitations

<!-- Assumptions, edge cases, what you would do with more data or compute. -->

- Not going for a "good" or generalizable model, just perfect fit for funsies
- Would run on way more data
- Non-stationarity would rear its head
- Person specific texting dynamics 
- Some sort of temporal/personal CV
- How to meaningfully parameterize texting conversations?
Issues:
    - Different people different params
    - Data goes back years, non stationairity
    - Daily cycle of life interrupts proper rhythm and assumptions of model
    - Combine double texting into a single text?
        - How to define double texting (some small timedelta?)
    

Choices/Evolution:
    - Filter out bots and one sided convos
    - Choose convo 78 with most data
    - Choose timeslice with most data 2022 Q4
    - Fit exponential hawke model, tails aren't well modeled so try powerlaw still not well modeled
        - Even with time of day regime splitting tails are significantly misfit.
    - Time of day regime splitting
        - Overall fit much improved
    - Optuna for hyperparam search for hawkes process fitting
        - still getting fat fails but better
    - Move away from regime splitting to a Hidden Markov Model Hawkes Process bc in some ways it simplifies things
        - Don't have to decide when to regime split
        - Don't have to think of ways to preserve time continuity based on regime splitting
    - Redid exponential plotting so that it actually worked
    - Redid powerlaw plotting too
    - Hidden Markov Model Process
        - Poor fit with expo kernel
        - Trying powerlaw kernel
        - Might assume independent events which is obviously violated
        - How does it "know" when to switch regimes? I'm not giving it any info about the actual conversation.
    - Diurnal Baseline Model
        - Barely improved things
        - One bump is obviously not enough based on EDA plots not sure why I even bothered with this when FFT exists
    - FFT Baseline Model
        - Small improvement but still less than I want


## Reproducibility

Github Repo
